title: palicourea subaeneofusca
intro: Palicourea subaeneofusca, also known as "Cinchona de monte," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America. It belongs to the Rubiaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the cardiovascular system and exhibit anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Palicourea subaeneofusca is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to aid in the treatment of malaria and other diseases.
